summary: Phase 2.B (live GitHub Pages verification of the Phase 2.A mapgen + ASCII preview deliverable) complete. The external acceptance criteria that were unreachable from the sandbox during Phase 2.A have all now been observed green on the public GitHub Actions run for commit 394801f on master. With Phase 2.0, 2.A, and 2.B all approved, Phase 2 (Map Generation) is complete and Phase 3 (Entity Model, Turn Loop, Combat) is unblocked.
suggested_commit_message: phase-2.B: live deploy + cross-runtime Playwright preview verified green

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm the Phase 2.A foundation has not drifted before approving the Phase 2 verification gate.
verification_sprint.unit_tests: 411 passed (vitest run --coverage); 100% lines / 100% statements / 100% functions on src/core/**, src/mapgen/**, src/registries/** (last verified locally at HEAD of phase-2.A).
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: Live URL https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ fetched (HTTP 200); deployed bundle index-CzKLE1eJ.js contains all expected runtime markers: self-test-banner, self-test passed, RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST, FLOOR_PREVIEW (Phase 2.A flag), Floor preview (UI heading), Phase 2 mapgen (UI subtitle), Generate floor (button label), preview-seed (input id), TILE_FLOOR (registry import). Deep-link form https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/#seed=phase2-witness&floor=3 also returns HTTP 200.

deploy_workflow_runs_green_within_5min.evidence: GitHub Actions run 25294232381 for commit 394801f on master: status=completed, conclusion=success. run_started_at 2026-05-03T23:43:43Z → updated_at 2026-05-03T23:45:22Z = 1m 39s end-to-end, well under the 5-minute cap. Both jobs (build & test, deploy to GitHub Pages) succeeded with all 17 build-test steps and 3 deploy steps green.

live_url_serves_updated_diagnostic_with_.evidence: curl -sSL https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ → 200 OK. index.html references /icefall/assets/index-CzKLE1eJ.js. Bundle contains all Phase 2.A markers: self-test-banner, self-test passed, RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST (Phase 1 carry-over), FLOOR_PREVIEW (window flag), Floor preview (h2 text), Phase 2 mapgen (preview subtitle), Generate floor (submit button label), preview-seed (input id), TILE_FLOOR (mapgen import). The render-time green-status assertion is also covered by the cross-runtime Playwright assertion below.

phase_2b_playwright_preview_assertions_g.evidence: Job 'build & test' step 'install playwright browsers' (chromium firefox webkit, --with-deps): success in 50s. Step 'cross-runtime e2e' (npm run test:e2e): success in 14s. playwright.config.ts defines projects chromium/firefox/webkit with forbidOnly:true and retries:0, so any single-project failure would have failed the step. tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts now contains six tests (4 from Phase 1.B carry-over + 2 added by Phase 2.A): self-test green; cross-runtime random-walk digest; build info DEV- fingerprint; Node-side digest match; 'floor preview UI is ready and renders an ASCII grid' (asserts window.__FLOOR_PREVIEW__ === 'ready' plus 60-char-wide rows); 'floor preview is deterministic — same seed/floor → identical ASCII' (loads /icefall/#seed=phase2-e2e&floor=3, captures window.__FLOOR_PREVIEW_ASCII__, clicks Generate floor, captures again, asserts equality). 6 tests × 3 engines = 18 cross-runtime assertions green.

bundle_size_budget_75kb.evidence: Job 'build & test' step 'bundle size budget (≤ 75 KB gzipped, memo decision 13)': success. The local Phase 2.A build measured the gzipped JS at ~11.5 KB (84% headroom). The CI gate uses `find dist/assets -name '*.js' -exec gzip -c {} \; | wc -c` against a 76800-byte threshold.

bundle_report_artifact_uploaded.evidence: Workflow artifact 'bundle-report' = 13,439 bytes, expired=false. Future phases reviewing budget bumps can download and inspect.

code_review_status: Phase 2.B did not produce new application code; the artifact under verification is the Phase 2.A code already reviewed and approved (artifacts/code-review-phase-2-A.md, verdict APPROVE WITH NITS, no blockers, nits N1/N2/N4/N5 addressed in the Phase 2.A commit). The CI run on commit 394801f also exercised the linter, typechecker, unit tests (411 passing), build, bundle-size gate, and Playwright suite (chromium + firefox + webkit) — every gate passed. No code-reviewer reinvocation required.
qa_playwright_status: Phase 2.B's qa-playwright equivalent is the CI Playwright job itself (the in-sandbox agent cannot download the Microsoft-hosted browser binaries; the cross-runtime CI job is the authoritative observation). It ran chromium + firefox + webkit on commit 394801f and reported green for the diagnostic suite (6 tests × 3 engines = 18 passing assertions, including the two new Phase 2.A floor-preview assertions).
follow_ups_carried_forward_from_phase_2A: code-review #N3: tools/gen-fixtures.ts lacks a dedicated unit test (transitively covered by the fixture-pack byte-equality tests; add a focused test before mapgen registries gain another writer in Phase 6).

code-review #N6: door-placement coverage is sparse on standard floors — 5 of 10 floor-1 fixtures have doors:[]; locked by the fixture pack so any fix is a rulesetVersion bump. Worth revisiting if door logic is touched.

code-review #N7: the decodeBase64Url helper inside src/mapgen/serialize.ts could relocate to src/core/hash.ts for symmetry with the encoder. Pure organizational cleanup; round-trip correctness is verified.
next_phase: Phase 3 — Entity Model, Turn Loop, Combat. Drift-detection sweep should fold in the three carried-forward Phase 2.A follow-ups (gen-fixtures unit test, door-placement coverage, decodeBase64Url relocation) as part of Phase 3's setup. Phase 3 builds src/sim/ on top of the deterministic mapgen output; the action descriptor schema (Phase 1 frozen contract 7) becomes load-bearing then. Phase 3 is a planning-gate phase per docs/PHASES.md and requires a decision memo + architecture-red-team review before any sim/ code is written.
